entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer and official Hermes Agent provider that manages user, session, and agent states. It utilizes hybrid retrieval through semantic, keyword, and entity matching and is available as a managed cloud or self-hosted Apache-2.0 project.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: An official Hermes Agent memory engine featuring a custom vector graph engine and context fencing for high-performance recall. It maintains persistent memory graphs and automated user profiling, sustaining sub-300ms recall speeds for massive token volumes.
entries.volcengine/OpenClaw: An official AGPL-3.0 context database that uses a filesystem-based organization and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading to reduce token consumption. It provides visualized retrieval trajectories for debugging and significantly improves task-completion rates over baselines.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated agent brain that automatically extracts typed entity relationships into a self-wiring knowledge graph. It includes a synthesis layer for cited prose answers and supports scoped data access for multi-user company environments.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system that implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent improvement. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper and supports a wide range of providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and local models.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: An official Hermes Agent provider that treats memory as a git repository, allowing for branching, merging, and versioning of context trees. It features a 5-tier retrieval system with sub-100ms latency and integrates with the Model Context Protocol.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations rather than simple text chunks. It tracks multi-peer perspectives to model what different entities know about one another via a FastAPI-based server.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving harness that distills successful agent strategies into reusable playbooks. It employs multi-role coordination for strategy curation and supports MCP server integration across multiple LLM providers.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system utilizing a SQLite backend to achieve sub-millisecond retrieval performance. It manages long-term knowledge through episodic compression and temporal triples, supporting Python SDKs and native agent plugins.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical summary DAG for depth-aware compaction. It preserves raw messages in a SQLite store and provides specialized tools like lcm_grep and lcm_expand_query for precise retrieval.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for the Honcho library that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports any OpenAI-compatible or local inference server and includes automated Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis setup scripts.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A local-first identity and secrets layer that provides portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients. It uses SQLite for storage and features a repairable system for editing or reclassifying agent context.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: An on-device context engine that eliminates cloud dependencies and API keys for local-first operation. It employs hybrid retrieval combining BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs via MCP servers and agent plugins.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer using an open YAML engram format to enable multi-agent systems without API costs. It utilizes ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts while decaying stale information through MCP tools.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases cache hits by 4–12× through prefix reordering and deduplication. It reduces prefill latency by 1.5–3× and provides native plugin support for the Hermes Agent and OpenClaw ecosystems.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: An embedded, self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable recall via why_retrieved tags. It handles contradiction tracking and canonicalization in-process, requiring no separate server or GPU.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It leverages a multi-layered storage approach using SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to provide bounded evidence packing and reduce prompt noise.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system combining BM25 full-text search with ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories, URLs, and multimedia files, using edge tags to create navigable relationships with inverse links.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses local-first storage with SQLite-vec and GGUF models, operating as an MCP-native server for coding agent clients.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt token overhead by using local BM25 ranking to select only relevant tool schemas. It includes a dashboard to track token savings and is compatible with Hermes Agent v0.14.0.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database and importance-weighted recall to surface high-priority memories. It supports BM25 keyword search and optional GLiNER2 entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures complete deletion of memories without leaving persistent summaries. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log for every operation and utilizes the Claude model family for on-demand summarization.
entries.futurebrowser/hermes-exabase-plugin: A plugin that integrates Exabase M-1 to provide self-organizing long-term memory. It offers tools for manual storage and searching, alongside configurable query expansion and result reranking.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first design and prototyping system that generates prototypes, decks, and motion graphics. It integrates with 21 local CLIs via MCP server installation and includes 150 design systems.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A library of 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It uses the agentskills.io standard to enable sub-second skill discovery across 26 security domains.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: Enables the generation of draw.io diagrams from natural language, featuring presets for ERD, UML, and Architecture. It provides automated structure diagrams for multiple languages and access to over 10,000 official shapes.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: An auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns through rewrite, detect, and edit modes. It employs a two-pass detection system and is compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, and Cursor.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across different agents and teams with native Hermes and OpenAI-compatible API integration.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library providing modular frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy. It is compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io agents via a marketplace or npx CLI.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically propose and generate reusable skills. It creates both Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation using dedicated slash commands.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Provides type-safe agentskills.io support for Pydantic AI using progressive disclosure to reduce token usage. It supports both programmatic and filesystem-based Python skill definitions.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A set of tools for extracting transcripts, captions, and timestamps from YouTube without using yt-dlp. It supports video search, channel browsing, and playlist extraction for Hermes Agent and other compatible agents.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing skills featuring automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace with crypto/Stripe payments and an agent-to-agent trust scoring system.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Teaches Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problems. It generates constraint reports and identifies structural conservation laws within codebases.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that provides structured memory, gated lifecycles, and a plan-approve-execute workflow. It stores work and proof in local plain files and integrates with Claude Code and Codex agents.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves prose, diagrams, and source code into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Implements cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories to help agents remember work. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting training data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A pre-build workflow that converts rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Provides agentskills.io compliant tools for interacting with the Chainlink oracle network and smart contracts. It supports CCIP cross-chain messaging, VRF v2.5 management, and Data Feed integration.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills. It supports version-pinned immutable skills and links instructions directly to specific file lines or URLs.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: Official FLUX image generation skills providing standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 models. It includes API integration patterns for webhooks and support for precise hex color specifications.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry of reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification featuring an OpenAPI-driven Python client. It supports versioned uploads with recursive dependency resolution and TON/Stripe payment rails.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: Facilitates the autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking. It includes built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption. It features a socket-based daemon for persistent server management and supports hot-reloading of JSON configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: A skill for managing Nextcloud files, notes, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It uses Nextcloud App Passwords for secure HTTPS communication and includes a guided setup script.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It utilizes SKILL.md files for progressive disclosure and integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool loading.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermes-multichannel-prompt-opt: A plugin that rewrites and scores prompts across various surfaces, including Discord, Telegram, and CLI. It reduces token usage by 20–80% by removing noise and tailoring structure to the specific model vendor.
entries.willingning-coder/eagle-eye: An intelligent retrieval system using a 5-layer approach including FTS5, dense embeddings, and RRF fusion. It filters hundreds of skills down to the five most relevant to reduce token consumption.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages for various AI coding agents and CLIs, including Hermes. It features automated daily updates to ensure users always have access to the most recent tool versions within a reproducible Nix environment.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: ClawPhone offers specialized scripts and configuration guides for running agent CLIs on ARM64 Android devices via Termux. It integrates with Termux:API and Termux:GUI, allowing Hermes and other OpenClaw agents to interact directly with smartphone hardware.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows desktop solution provides a GUI-driven Hermes experience with 100 built-in tools and local model support via LM Studio. It functions as a portable automation engine, allowing users to build custom workflow pipelines without requiring Docker or complex installations.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: Designed for mobile privacy and cost-efficiency, this project features a one-line Termux installer for running Hermes Agent locally on Android. It supports over 200 models, including local execution via Ollama and remote access through major API providers.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: This community Helm chart enables cloud-native deployment of Hermes Agent on Kubernetes with a focus on state safety. It enforces single-writer storage patterns and offers configurable gateway modes, making it suitable for operator-ready production workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw provides a high-security sandbox for Hermes Agent using kernel-level enforcement to restrict network, filesystem, and process access. It allows users to apply dynamic security policies while integrating the agent with IDEs, MCP servers, and messaging platforms.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent in a sandboxed environment. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific versions of Hermes using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, including systemd service configuration and secure secrets handling via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between Nix-managed reproducibility and interactive CLI usage for a hybrid deployment model.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Focused on production-ready headless operation, this tool configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is specifically designed for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It features workspace isolation, task scheduling, and inter-agent delegation, providing an enterprise-grade management layer for complex agent hierarchies.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack optimized for Portainer, featuring a ttyd web terminal for remote browser-based access. It simplifies management through a Git Repository stack and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration layer that allows users to dispatch tasks and monitor spending across multi-agent workflows. It features a dependency-free SQLite stack and the Aegis review system to ensure high-quality task gates across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A management surface providing TUI and PWA dashboards to monitor various coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It ensures secure execution through Docker, Podman, and Apple Container sandboxing while utilizing git worktrees for parallel agent operations.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: A control-room template designed to scale Hermes deployments from a single VPS agent to complex specialist teams. It implements a sidecar control plane for governance and utilizes task bus patterns for structured delegation.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: A supervision hub that lets users create and review autonomous Hermes Agent work via a centralized screen. It features a Kanban board for status tracking and live streaming of agent reasoning for human-in-the-loop verification.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: A framework for building autonomous agent swarms with support for over 23 LLM providers, including Ollama and Hermes. It manages swarms through heartbeats and schedules, featuring durable memory and the ability to learn new skills from conversations.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: A scaling template that uses a level-based architecture to transition from single agents to automated teams. It includes a task bus for handoffs and bundled skills for VPS provisioning, security auditing, and backup management.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local collaboration system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols. It provides a real-time Web UI to monitor agent dialogues and manage the task lifecycle via Hermes Kanban.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized collective of 17 agents dedicated to research, planning, and implementation. The system enforces strict quality gates through mandatory reviewer and tester agents to secure infrastructure and refactoring pipelines.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization harness that uses automated outer-loop searches to refine agent code. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track performance frontiers through structured mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It orchestrates nine agents using Paperclip and Hermes Agent, visualizing the competition via a real-time scoreboard and task dashboard.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: An ACP-style delegation tool that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It prevents parent context pollution through isolated workflows and enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output limits.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: A local pre-indexed code knowledge graph that optimizes agent performance by mapping symbol relationships and call graphs. It significantly lowers operational costs and token consumption by reducing the number of tool calls required for code navigation.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust-based TUI designed to visualize and track token usage across various AI coding agents. It aggregates data from JSONL files and local databases to provide a centralized view of costs for Hermes and other clients.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: A local trace viewer that allows developers to inspect API traffic, prompts, and tool calls from agents like Hermes. It generates portable HTML artifacts, making it easy to archive and share reconstructed streaming responses for debugging.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A collection of community-driven CLI themes that customize the Hermes terminal UI's visual identity. Users can apply diverse presets or create custom skins to modify ASCII art, banner colors, and response labels via YAML configurations.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated orchestration layer that streamlines the entire app lifecycle from building to monitoring. It integrates 23 specialized skills to automate GitHub issue triage, security reviews, and deployments via Vercel and Supabase.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A native port of the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. This integration eliminates the need for OAuth or external processes while adding MLOps tools for RL training and vLLM inference.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control through MCP integration. It features a supervisory interface that allows humans to approve actions, roll back changes, and manage durable sessions with structured checkpoints.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A TAW Agent framework that enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents with per-folder configurations. It allows developers to seamlessly switch between a default agent and project-specific agents with local memories and custom instructions.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: A preconfigured setup utilizing RTK and Caveman templating to achieve massive token savings of up to 99%. It provides an automated installation process and over 40 specialized skills for research, finance, and devops.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that scans agent configurations and system prompts for failure modes without using LLM calls. It utilizes seven structural detectors and HERM v1.1 scoring to integrate quality checks directly into CI/CD pipelines.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A rapid deployment stack that uses Docker to launch Hermes Agent alongside LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It provides a zero-cost entry point by automating the configuration of free model routing and 29 community plugins.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A dedicated migration utility that transfers personas, memories, and workspace files from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It maps complex model configurations and handles the transfer of credentials from system-level environment files.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: A cross-platform Tauri-based manager that provides a unified desktop interface for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and other CLI agents. It simplifies the management of multiple agent tools across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: A local cowork application that integrates Hermes Agent and 13+ other CLI agents into a single GUI. It features a built-in engine for web search and MCP tools, with the ability to automatically generate editable Office documents.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A lightweight web-based interface built with Python and vanilla JavaScript that maintains full feature parity with the Hermes CLI. It utilizes a three-panel layout for efficient workspace file browsing and inline previews.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app designed for the installation and configuration of local or remote Hermes backends. It includes a GUI for managing personas and scheduled tasks, alongside integration with 16 messaging gateways.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A web dashboard focused on multi-platform configuration and session management across eight messaging channels like Discord and Slack. It offers isolated profiles for memory and model visibility, paired with a web terminal for remote management.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace featuring a dedicated inspector panel and memory browser for vanilla Hermes Agent installations. It introduces Swarm Mode to orchestrate multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A macOS-native workspace that connects directly to Hermes hosts via SSH. It provides a professional developer experience with real session data, an embedded shell terminal, and native Kanban views.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted control plane featuring the ZOO Swarm Monitor for live event tracking and pipeline management. It provides comprehensive MCP server control, including a config editor and real-time tool call cards.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A macOS session browser and cockpit that provides local-only indexing of AI agent histories without telemetry. It allows users to search through multiple agent logs and resume CLI sessions via terminal emulators.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: A macOS interface specifically optimized for Hermes Agent instances running on Orgo cloud VMs and SSH hosts. It features one-click cloud installation and WebRTC voice mode via OpenAI Realtime.
entries.liftaris/herm: An operator-focused TUI built with OpenTUI for chat and profile switching. It includes a native Eikon Marketplace for installing shared extensions and a Kanban board for monitoring agentic tasks.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: A Windows-first desktop application built with Rust and Tauri, featuring a built-in Hermes Agent kernel. It provides integrated health checks and management tools for MCP skills, memory, and profiles.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed as a Telegram Mini App with a terminal-style UI. It implements secure HMAC-SHA256 authentication and supports vision analysis for file attachments.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows portable GUI that bundles over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and TTS, without requiring Docker or admin rights. It includes a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic React 18 web interface that uses a lightweight Python proxy for self-hosting. It provides real-time log streaming and a live workspace file browser for managing skills and cron jobs.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring and configuration dashboard focused on transparency and analysis. It features cost tracking, model distribution metrics, and full-text search for browsing conversation histories.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace featuring a chat-first interface with tool approval cards and SSE streaming. It includes a dedicated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and isolated profile management for API keys.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: A multi-agent desktop client that orchestrates collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents. It features a visual Skill Store with 50+ one-click installations and a PM orchestrator for task management.
